mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative equity repricing within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.0, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving sustained positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production indicate immediate supply-side bottlenecks that force rapid equity sell-offs within one week during inflationary periods.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in 60 percent of INDPRO contraction months, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL growth falls below 2 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates above 4.5 percent impose severe financing constraints on highly leveraged sectors, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 during high-rate environments, or 20-day returns show no negative skew when FEDFUNDS exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor absorption that supports corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns over three months.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in over 50 percent of triggered months, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth rates above 2 percent reflect sustained economic expansion that immediately boosts equity valuations within the following trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in 60 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2 percent, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 105 indicates severe manufacturing weakness that triggers negative equity repricing over 20 days when monetary policy remains restrictive.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn positive in 65 percent of cases when INDPRO drops below 105, or the mechanism fails to generate a negative Rank IC during high-rate periods.
